original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment — Recommendations
original_registry_entry.version: NICE NG125; last updated 2020-08-19; last reviewed 2023-05-31
original_registry_entry.population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for postoperative wound-care and surgical-site-infection documentation; partial for traumatic wounds, suture removal, and UAE pathways.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.4 — information, wound care, and infection recognition
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented postoperative wound-care information, infection-warning discussion, and who to contact if concern develops.

exact_sections.heading: Overview and recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.8
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines the postoperative surgical-wound population and supports wound dressing, cleansing, wound-healing, and structured postoperative wound-care documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.9–1.4.11 and surgical-site-infection definition
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports review of suspected infection, cellulitis, microbiology context, structured wound care, and local heat, redness, pain, swelling, discharge, separation, abscess, fever, or systemic signs.

title: Surgical site infections: prevention and treatment — Recommendations
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
population: Adults, young people, and children having a surgical procedure involving a skin incision.
intended_setting: Perioperative, postoperative, community, and specialist wound-care services.
